what_it_does: This file is the Company's service registry: a self-describing JSON catalog that lists every managed service, its group, port, management unit or script, resource budgets, configuration, and named loadout combos, plus variant inheritance rules, so the `company` command can discover, budget, and start/stop on-demand services.
observations: No boot autostart: `autostart` only means included in bare `company up`; everything else is explicit.
Services are grouped into core, brain, voice, models, reach, and jobs.
Each service carries `vram_mb` and `ram_mb` estimates for resource budgeting against a 16 GB GPU ceiling.
Combos declare concrete co-resident loadouts; variants use `extends`, `swap`, `add`, and `remove` to derive loadouts.
Management types vary: user-unit, system-unit, manual script, and hosted Supabase edge function.
Some services are custom script-based servers (`serve`) rather than config-driven vLLM units.
Notes and `_doc` fields embed historical decisions, verification dates, and caveats directly in the registry.
The registry distinguishes the live interactive surface (canvas, bridge, remote) from on-demand models and scheduled jobs.
standouts: Variant loadout mechanism (`extends`/`swap`/`add`/`remove`) turns combos into an extensible class hierarchy.
Resource-first design: every service exposes `vram_mb`/`ram_mb` and combos sum co-resident footprints.
Explicit no-autostart policy encoded in metadata rather than systemd defaults.
Self-documenting: `_doc` blocks and per-entry `note` fields carry rationale, dates, and preconditions.
conventions: JSON is both data and documentation: long `_doc` and `note` strings explain intent inline.
Services use stable identifiers as keys and carry human `title` plus machine `manage` blocks.
Resource estimates are approximate and explicitly labeled as estimates, not measurements.
Combos are named sets; variants inherit and mutate via small deltas.
Dates and author attributions are recorded in notes for traceability.
Config-driven vLLM services share a common shape (`model`, `port`, `gpu_util`, `flags`, `env`).
concerns: Some `vram_mb`/`ram_mb` figures are estimates that may drift from actual measured usage.
A few entries mix operational metadata with runtime configuration (`config` blocks) in the same file.
Inconsistent GPU cost claims: `stt-whisper` and `stt-moonshine` notes say 0 VRAM but `vram_mb` is non-zero.
Port reuse across services (`embed-jina-v5` and `embed-qwen3` both claim port 8004) could cause runtime conflicts.
Some `config.model` values are empty or marked unverified (e.g., `tts-kokoro`).
Combo variant inheritance is resolved externally (`registry.combos()`), so this file alone does not validate cycles or missing bases.
notes: A resource-budgeted, self-documenting service manifest that encodes not just what runs, but how loadouts inherit and why certain decisions were made.
questions: Who or what enforces that combo VRAM sums stay under `vram_ceiling_mb`?
Where is `registry.combos()` implemented and how are cycles or missing bases reported?
How are port conflicts between services like `embed-jina-v5` and `embed-qwen3` prevented?
Are `vram_mb` values updated automatically or manually when model configs change?
What consumes the `serve` scripts and how does `company swap` rewrite default models?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it is a single self-describing source of truth for services, loadouts, groups, and resource budgets, exactly as the top-level `_doc` claims.
apparent_themes: On-demand, explicit service lifecycle over boot-time autostart.
Resource-aware orchestration with GPU/RAM budgets as first-class metadata.
Self-describing infrastructure as code and documentation.
Loadout classes and inheritance for combinatorial service variants.
Traceability through dated, attributed decision notes.
intention_signals: The author is trying to turn an ad-hoc collection of systemd units, vLLM servers, voice engines, and scheduled jobs into a single, readable, budgeted control plane that a human operator and an automation tool can agree on, while preserving the history and rationale behind each service.
novelty: Embedding loadout variant inheritance (`extends`/`swap`/`add`/`remove`) and per-service resource budgets inside a single JSON service registry, making the file act as both operational catalog and lightweight class system.
contribution: It contributes a unified, human-and-machine-readable inventory of services, loadouts, resource estimates, and lifecycle intent that a fused system can use to reason about what to start, what fits, and why each service exists.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON service registry for the Company project listing managed services, their groups, ports, systemd or script management, GPU/RAM budgets, configuration, named loadout combos with variant inheritance, and inline decision documentation.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single self-describing source of truth that the `company` command and operators use to discover, budget, and start on-demand services and loadouts.
